question: Can the fixed 20/30/60/90-calendar-day BTCUSDT long-only Donchian rule become materially more robust when signal, trailing exit and rebalance updates move from daily to native 8h bars?
intended_claim: product consideration for an existing Demo instrument only after all sequential gates
known_exposure: BTC price history and the daily version of this rule have already been viewed. The 8h candidate is a literature-inspired mechanism transfer with twelve-trial correction and sealed sequential time gates, not virgin-data discovery.

external_design_basis.intermediate_frequency_and_trailing_exit: Bui and Nguyen 2026 reports H6/H8 above D1 and trailing-stop importance
external_design_basis.excluded_complexity: No rolling optimization, RSI, Sharpe-ranked universe, market-cap filter, 150-coin portfolio or multi-million parameter search

fixed_candidate.exit: component midpoint trailing exit updated every 8h and never moved against the position

benchmarks: same fixed long-only Donchian rule updated only daily

selection_bias.columns: six BTC daily Donchian, three BTC carry-conditioned, one BTC Spot transfer, one ETH transfer, one BTC 8h transfer

development_gate: base and stress total returns positive
at least two of 2021/2022/2023 positive and worst year at least -3%
base Sharpe and Calmar both exceed daily execution of the same rule
evaluation_gate: base and stress total returns positive
base Sharpe and Calmar both exceed daily execution of the same rule
confirmation_gate: base and stress total returns non-negative
evaluation plus confirmation base and stress CAGR above 4%
allowed_fixes_after_checkpoint: Only code, serialization, data-integrity or documentation corrections that preserve the fixed rule, periods, costs, related-trial accounting and gates. Record fixes and refresh code hash before opening another phase.
opening_rule: Run development only. Evaluation requires evaluation_authorized=true; confirmation requires confirmation_authorized=true.
